bullets.text: At least 27 people were killed in a bar fire in Bangkok, Thailand, with firefighters reporting patrons running through flames to escape the blaze.
The US insists the Strait of Hormuz remains open despite exchanging strikes with Iran, after Iranian forces reportedly declared the strait closed and attacked US allies and bases.
China has evacuated nearly two million people as a powerful typhoon made landfall, leading to the suspension of schools, work, outdoor activities, and many transport services in Zhejiang province.
A father jailed for abusing two children is appealing his conviction, arguing that evidence given by the multiple personalities of the same witness was unlawful.
A crackdown on illegal camping in Noosa, prompted by hundreds of complaints, has sparked outrage locally, with many arguing fines will not solve the issue.
Many aged care residents in Australia are reportedly being charged for services they cannot use or understand, prompting an investigation from the regulator.
The US dollar's strength is creating challenges for the bond market, prompting investors to adapt to this new dynamic.
New Zealand plans to finalize a contract for its first liquefied natural gas import facility before the upcoming November election, anticipating lower global LNG prices.
The World Bank has reportedly abandoned its climate targets, signaling a shift in the institution's focus on green growth initiatives.
